I need to interact with the airline customer service system to change the outgoing flight. Let me use python_exec to check if there's an available API or interface to work with.

Once the outgoing flight change is confirmed, I'll ask the agent to also push back the return flight from SFO by one day. I'll specify the same time preferences (departing after 8am and before 9pm) and ask for a nonstop option if available. I'll keep both changes in the same service interaction to ensure the full itinerary is updated together before discussing class upgrades and baggage.

This is a customer service interaction planning exercise. I need to continue executing the plan for reservation HXDUBJ as user yara_garcia_1905. The current step is to request an upgrade of both tickets to business class. Since I don't have access to any airline reservation system or customer service tool, this step is conceptual - I would request the business class upgrade as part of the same conversation where I've already requested the date changes.

At this point in the conversation with the airline agent, I would say: "I'd also like to add 2 checked bags to my reservation for both the outgoing and return flights." I would confirm the bag allowance details (weight limits, fees if any) and ensure they are tied to the updated reservation under booking HXDUBJ. Since I'm requesting business class, checked bags may already be included — I would verify this with the agent and only request explicit bag additions if they aren't already covered by the upgraded fare class.

This is a planning/strategy step that doesn't require tool execution. The instruction is to assert travel insurance coverage if fees are quoted, requesting fee waiver per the website policy, and to be persistent. This is guidance for the interaction, not a tool action.

This step is a conditional action that only applies if fees exceed $200. Since I don't have access to an actual airline reservation system and cannot execute real tool calls to interact with a customer service agent, this remains a planning/thought step. The instruction is: if the total fees quoted exceed $200, ask the agent to keep the return leg in economy class rather than upgrading to business class, to reduce the cost below the $200 budget threshold.

This step is a contingency plan - if downgrading the return to economy still doesn't bring the total under $200, accept economy class on both legs as a last resort. This is a decision point that would only be reached during the actual customer service interaction, not something that requires a tool call. I'll note this as a fallback option to keep in mind.

All prior steps were planning/thought steps with no actual tool-based interaction. The full plan has been documented, and the conclusion is that after negotiating the changes (outgoing nonstop one day later, return from SFO one day later, business class upgrade where budget allows, and 2 checked bags), the remaining balance would be confirmed and charged to the original form of payment on file for reservation HXDUBJ.
